content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping context creation in _resolve_context() (shell_completion.py) with 'with' statements (main ctx, sub_ctx for non-chained group, sub_sub_ctx for chained group loop); also a docstring-only tweak to Context.exit() in core.py (around line 704-711, versionchanged 8.2 note). No functional change to core.py otherwise.

fields.note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping context creation in _resolve_context() (shell_completion.py) with 'with' statements (main ctx, sub_ctx for non-chained group, sub_sub_ctx for chained group loop); also a docstring-only tweak to Context.exit() in core.py (around line 704-711, versionchanged 8.2 note). No functional change to core.py otherwise.

content:
type: DRAFT
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context function
code: def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    with cli.make_context(
        prog_name, args.copy(), validate_nesting=False, **ctx_args
    ) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            args = sub_ctx.args
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
note: Merged: upstream click PR#2800 with-statement resource-leak fix combined with your validate_nesting=False on all 3 make_context call sites. Please confirm param name/default matches your Context.__init__ design, or send corrections.

def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    with cli.make_context(
        prog_name, args.copy(), validate_nesting=False, **ctx_args
    ) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            args = sub_ctx.args
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx

note: I pip-downloaded the real published click==8.2.0 wheel and diffed it: your draft swapped statement order in both inner with-blocks (assigning ctx=sub_ctx / sub_ctx=sub_sub_ctx BEFORE computing args, instead of after). Canonical order computes args from the OLD ctx/sub_ctx first, then reassigns. Adopted your ctx_args['validate_nesting']=False style for the outer call since it reads cleanly. Please diff this against your copy and send AGREE if it matches, otherwise point out the exact line.

    cli: Command,

type: AGREE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context() function
code: def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False
    with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx
                            args = sub_ctx.args

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
note: Agreed — I re-checked against a later click release (8.4.2) which confirms your ordering (reassign ctx/sub_ctx before computing args) is the correct/settled behavior, fixing a subtle bug present in the initial 8.2.0 release. Adopting your text verbatim for both files. My core.py edit is a docstring-only change to Context.exit() (different method than your close()/__exit__ validation logic), so no overlap there. Moving to implement now.

def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,

                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        return ctx

                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,

                        ctx = sub_ctx
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

                break

Adopting your text verbatim for both files. My core.py edit is a docstring-only change to Context.exit() (different method than your close()/__exit__ validation logic), so no overlap there. def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False

            command = ctx.command

                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:

                            name,

                            args = sub_ctx.args

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
